title: Dermatology Outpatient — Radio Button & First RESULTTASK Row
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage
schemaFilter: Q1 → column selector | Q3=No
clinicalContext: A dermatology consultant sees an outpatient and orders a chest X-ray. Report must reach the consultant who saw the patient. In an outpatient encounter the ordering clinician and the responsible consultant are the same person.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting MIS Delivery Params — Radio Button ★ Attending (Trust-wide default from S1) ★ Requesting Clinician (Trust-wide default from S1) Re-notify on Addendum NO (set here; changed in S22) RESULTTASK row ★ IMG.DERM (first row created) Deliver To ★ Provider Only Priority Handling Standard Result Task Generation None Workgroup Dictionary No workgroup — Provider Only Ancillary Mapping Not yet configured (S3) User Override None Surveillance Rules None
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Dermatology Consultant Radio Button = Attending → consultant is the Attending on OP encounter. Report in personal inbox. Radio Button = Requesting → consultant is the ordering clinician on OP encounter. Report in personal inbox. IDENTICAL outcome in outpatient setting.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Silent ⚠ No failure — consultant is always Attending on their own OP encounter. No failure — consultant is always the requesting clinician on their own OP encounter. Displaced If a locum consultant sees the patient but the original consultant is listed as Attending through an admin error. If a secretary places the order on behalf of the consultant — the result routes to the secretary's inbox.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B Ensure that whoever sees the patient in outpatients is the Attending (A) or the ordering clinician (B) in the EPR. Admin ordering on behalf of clinicians is a governance risk under Path B.

asNote: S1 sets the Radio Button Trust-wide. This is the most important single configuration decision in the entire system. Correct terminology in Expanse PP64: the field is labelled 'Attending' — NOT 'Attending/ED' (MAGIC-era label). This distinction matters when navigating the MIS Customer Defined Parameters screen.
keyLearning: S1 is the foundation scenario. The Radio Button setting (Attending vs Requesting Clinician) applies Trust-wide and cannot be changed per service. In outpatient settings where the consultant both sees the patient AND orders investigations, Paths A and B produce identical outcomes. The difference emerges in S2 and beyond when these roles diverge.

title: AMU — CT Head: Primary Recipient Diverges
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage
schemaFilter: Q1 → column selector (this is the scenario where A and B first produce different outcomes) | Q3=No
clinicalContext: A medical registrar on AMU orders a CT head. The admitting medical consultant is the Attending. The report should reach the consultant responsible for the patient — not the registrar who happened to place the order. Under Path A this happens automatically. Under Path B it does not.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting MIS Delivery Params — Radio Button Attending (from S1) Requesting Clinician (from S1) Re-notify on Addendum NO (from S1) RESULTTASK row ★ IMG.AMU Deliver To Provider Only Priority Handling Standard Result Task Generation None Workgroup Dictionary No workgroup — Provider Only Ancillary Mapping Not yet configured (S3) User Override None Surveillance Rules None
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Medical Consultant (Attending) Consultant is Attending → report in consultant's inbox. Consultant is NOT the requesting clinician → no notification under Provider Only. Ordering Registrar Registrar is not the Attending → no notification under Provider Only. Registrar is the requesting clinician → report in registrar's inbox.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Silent ⚠ Attending field is blank (patient not yet formally admitted) — no delivery target. Registrar rotates or leaves before report is authorised — result stranded in inactive inbox. Displaced None under standard operation. Registrar receives result but is no longer responsible for patient — incorrect clinical context for action.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A Ensure Attending field is populated at admission. Implement workgroup fallback (S5) to catch cases where Attending is blank. Path B Implement Result Task Generation to duplicate result to Attending (S6) OR use Workgroup + Provider pattern (S5) to ensure team coverage when individuals rotate.

asNote: S2 is where Path A and Path B first diverge in outcome. In outpatient settings (S1) the Attending and Requesting clinician are the same person. In acute inpatient settings they are not. This scenario demonstrates why Trust-wide Radio Button selection must be made with full understanding of your dominant service model. You cannot configure some services on Path A and others on Path B — the Radio Button applies globally.
keyLearning: S2 is the critical decision point. If your Trust predominantly uses a consultant-led model where the Attending is always the correct recipient, choose Path A. If your Trust's workflow relies heavily on ordering clinician responsibility (e.g., GP orders, AHP-led pathways), choose Path B. Neither is inherently better — the correct choice depends on your organisational structure and clinical governance model.

title: Surgical Assessment — Critical Finding Priority Escalation
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Ancillary Mapping (OBX-8)
schemaFilter: Q1 → column selector | Q3=No
clinicalContext: A surgical registrar orders a CT abdomen. The radiologist reports a ruptured AAA and flags it critical in CRIS, sending OBX-8 = 'C' in the HL7 ORU message. The result must reach the correct clinician at STAT priority.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting MIS Delivery Params — Radio Button Attending (from S1) Requesting Clinician (from S1) Re-notify on Addendum NO (from S1) RESULTTASK row ★ IMG.SURG Deliver To Provider Only Priority Handling ★ Use Ancillary Mapping (OBX-8) Result Task Generation None Workgroup Dictionary No workgroup — Provider Only Ancillary Mapping ★ OBX-8 = 'C' → STAT | OBX-8 = 'U' → Urgent | OBX-8 = 'A' → Abnormal | Default → Standard User Override None Surveillance Rules None
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Surgical Consultant (Attending) Consultant is Attending → STAT priority result in consultant's inbox with red flag. Consultant is NOT the requesting clinician → no notification under Provider Only. Critical finding not seen. Ordering Registrar Registrar is not the Attending → no notification under Provider Only. Registrar is the requesting clinician → STAT priority result in registrar's inbox with red flag.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Silent ⚠ Attending blank at time of authorisation — STAT result has no delivery target. CRITICAL patient safety incident risk. Registrar has rotated — STAT result stranded in inactive inbox. Higher risk than standard because critical finding is time-sensitive. Displaced None. Registrar receives STAT but is no longer caring for patient.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A MANDATORY: Implement workgroup fallback (S5) for STAT/Urgent results. If Attending is blank, STAT results MUST go to a monitored team inbox. Configure separate high-priority monitoring procedure for workgroup STAT results. Path B Implement Result Task Generation to duplicate STAT results to Attending (S6) OR use dual-delivery pattern (Workgroup + Provider) with OOH monitoring coverage. Consider direct pager/alert integration for STAT results.

asNote: Ancillary Mapping is configured ONCE Trust-wide and applies to ALL subsequent RESULTTASK rows automatically. The OBX-8 mapping values must be confirmed with your CRIS/RIS team — the exact codes sent depend on the radiology system. Common values: C=Critical, U=Unexpected/Significant, A=Abnormal. Do NOT assume values — confirm before go-live. Navigate to: MIS > Customer Defined Parameters > Result Notification > Ancillary Mapping.
keyLearning: S3 introduces Priority Handling via Ancillary Mapping. Critical findings (STAT) magnify the consequences of every failure mode from S2. If a standard result is missed it is dangerous — if a STAT result is missed it is potentially catastrophic. Compensation strategies become MANDATORY for STAT results, not optional. Many Trusts implement a 'Critical Results Phone Call' policy alongside electronic notification for this reason.

title: Emergency Department — Chest X-ray: ED Attending vs Requesting
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Ancillary Mapping
schemaFilter: Q1 → column selector | Q3=No | Q7 may apply (OOH operation)
clinicalContext: An ED junior doctor orders a chest X-ray for a patient with query pneumonia. The ED consultant is the Attending. The report comes back showing consolidation. Who receives the result depends on the Radio Button — and in ED settings, the clinical workflow implications differ from inpatient wards.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting MIS Delivery Params — Radio Button Attending (from S1) Requesting Clinician (from S1) Re-notify on Addendum NO (from S1) RESULTTASK row ★ IMG.ED Deliver To Provider Only Priority Handling Use Ancillary Mapping (from S3) Result Task Generation None Workgroup Dictionary No workgroup — Provider Only Ancillary Mapping From S3 (applies Trust-wide) User Override None Surveillance Rules None
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To ED Consultant (Attending) Consultant is Attending → result in consultant's inbox. Consultant is NOT the requesting clinician → no notification under Provider Only. Ordering Junior Doctor Junior doctor is not the Attending → no notification under Provider Only. Junior doctor is the requesting clinician → result in their inbox.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Silent ⚠ Attending field blank at triage — result has no delivery target. Common in ED during peak times. Junior doctor shift ends before report is authorised — result stranded in inactive inbox. Extremely common in ED due to rapid staff turnover. Displaced Patient seen by different consultant after handover — result goes to previous consultant who no longer has patient context. Junior doctor receives result after their shift ends, days later — no clinical context, patient likely discharged or admitted.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A MANDATORY: Implement ED workgroup fallback (S5). All ED imaging results should ALSO go to monitored ED team inbox. Ensure handover process includes review of pending results. Consider time-based routing (S8) for overnight results. Path B MANDATORY: Implement ED workgroup fallback (S5). Junior doctor rotation in ED is too frequent for individual-only delivery to be safe. Consider Result Task Generation (S6) to also notify Attending. Implement 'Pending ED Results' report for discharged patients.

asNote: S4 demonstrates why ED is a special case. The combination of: (1) rapid staff turnover, (2) patients frequently discharged before results authorise, (3) 24/7 operation, and (4) high-acuity critical findings means that NEITHER Path A nor Path B is safe in ED without additional compensation mechanisms. S5 (workgroup fallback) is considered MANDATORY for ED by most Trusts. Some Trusts configure a dedicated IMG.ED.DISCHARGED row to handle the specific case of patients discharged before results authorise — this routes to a designated 'ED Follow-up' inbox.
keyLearning: S4 exposes the limitations of pure individual-based routing in high-throughput, rapid-turnover environments. ED is the scenario that often drives Trusts to implement team-based (workgroup) routing patterns. If your schema Q7 answer was 'B' (OOH/rotation requirements), ED configuration becomes significantly more complex and requires Level 2 mechanisms immediately.

title: GP-Referred Outpatient MRI — Workgroup + Provider Dual Delivery
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Ancillary Mapping · Workgroup Dictionary
schemaFilter: Q1 → column selector | Q3=B (team inbox alongside individual)
clinicalContext: A GP refers a patient for an outpatient rheumatology MRI spine. The rheumatology consultant orders the scan. The result should go to BOTH the individual consultant AND the rheumatology team inbox for service-wide visibility and failsafe backup.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting MIS Delivery Params — Radio Button Attending (from S1) Requesting Clinician (from S1) Re-notify on Addendum NO (from S1) RESULTTASK row ★ IMG.RHEUM Deliver To ★ Workgroup + Provider (dual delivery pattern) Priority Handling Use Ancillary Mapping (from S3) Result Task Generation None Workgroup Dictionary ★ WG.RHEUM.IMAGING → maps to Expanse Workgroup 'Rheumatology Imaging Team' Ancillary Mapping From S3 (applies Trust-wide) User Override None Surveillance Rules None
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Rheumatology Consultant Consultant is Attending → result in consultant's personal inbox. Consultant is requesting clinician → result in consultant's personal inbox. Rheumatology Team Inbox Workgroup delivery active → result ALSO in WG.RHEUM.IMAGING shared inbox. Workgroup delivery active → result ALSO in WG.RHEUM.IMAGING shared inbox.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Silent ⚠ MITIGATED: If Attending blank, result still goes to workgroup. Failsafe achieved. MITIGATED: If requesting clinician inactive, result still goes to workgroup. Failsafe achieved. Displaced MITIGATED: Even if individual delivery is displaced, workgroup inbox ensures result visibility. MITIGATED: Even if individual delivery is displaced, workgroup inbox ensures result visibility. Duplicate INTRODUCED: Consultant receives result in personal inbox AND sees it in team inbox. Potential for inbox noise. INTRODUCED: Consultant receives result in personal inbox AND sees it in team inbox. Potential for inbox noise. Orphaned NEW RISK: Workgroup inbox exists but no one monitors it — functionally equivalent to silent failure. MANDATORY workgroup monitoring governance required. NEW RISK: Workgroup inbox exists but no one monitors it — functionally equivalent to silent failure. MANDATORY workgroup monitoring governance required.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B MANDATORY governance: Define WHO monitors the workgroup inbox, HOW OFTEN it is checked, and what the escalation process is for unactioned results. Document this in a Workgroup Monitoring SOP. Consider automated reporting for 'Results in Workgroup Inbox > X days unactioned'. The workgroup is not a black hole — it requires active management. Path A/B Implement naming convention for workgroups: WG.[SERVICE].[FUNCTION]. Create workgroups BEFORE configuring RESULTTASK rows. Train users that workgroup delivery is ADDITIONAL to personal delivery, not a replacement. Educate on the purpose: failsafe backup, not primary workflow.

asNote: S5 introduces the Workgroup Dictionary. Navigate to: MIS > Customer Defined Parameters > Result Notification > Workgroup Dictionary. Each entry maps a mnemonic (e.g., WG.RHEUM.IMAGING) to an Expanse Workgroup. The Expanse Workgroup must already exist in Workgroup Management before you can reference it here. When 'Deliver To' is set to 'Workgroup + Provider', the system delivers to BOTH the individual (per Radio Button) AND the workgroup. This is the most common failsafe pattern. 'Workgroup Only' is also possible but requires careful governance (S19).
keyLearning: S5 is the foundation of safe, resilient result delivery. Workgroup + Provider dual delivery provides redundancy: if the individual fails (rotation, blank field, inactive inbox), the workgroup catches the result. However, it introduces a NEW failure mode: the Orphaned workgroup. Every workgroup inbox must have documented monitoring governance. This is non-negotiable. S5 completes Level 1 maturity — all scenarios from here forward build on these five foundational patterns.

title: Virtual Fracture Clinic — Pathway via Result Task Generation
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Result Task Generation · Workgroup Dictionary
schemaFilter: Q4=Yes (pathway recipients) → EXPANDS to S6–S18
clinicalContext: An ED doctor orders an X-ray. The VFC team needs the result for next-day triage. The primary ED recipient and the VFC pathway recipient are different people. Task Generation creates an additional task to the VFC workgroup alongside the standard delivery.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending (from S1) Requesting Clinician (from S1) RESULTTASK row IMG.ED Deliver To Provider Only Result Task Generation — Condition ★ Visit Type = VFC → additional task to E.VFC (Trust-wide condition from this point) Task Generation — Target ★ E.VFC (ACPs, ortho registrars) E.VFC Triage Type ★ AMBRESULT (VFC patients are outpatient/ambulatory) Priority Handling Standard Workgroup Dictionary E.VFC (created for VFC pathway) Ancillary Mapping From S3 (Trust-wide) User Override None Surveillance Rules None
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Primary ED recipient ED Attending — standard RESULTTASK. ED requesting clinician — standard RESULTTASK. E.VFC Workgroup Task Generation fires on Visit Type = VFC. Workgroup receives result regardless of Radio Button setting. Same — Task Generation is independent of Radio Button. IDENTICAL workgroup outcome.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Silent ⚠ Visit Type field not populated as 'VFC' in the EPR — condition never fires, VFC team not notified. Same. Displaced None for VFC path. ED requesting clinician rotates before report authorised — same S4 rotation risk. Duplicate None (VFC workgroup and ED primary are different recipient sets) Orphaned E.VFC workgroup has no active members (covered in S41)
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B Task Generation conditions are independent of the Radio Button — they fire based on patient attributes, not on who ordered. This means workgroup compensations added here benefit both Path A and Path B organisations equally. Path A/B Establish a governance process: who is responsible for tagging a patient as VFC in the EPR? If this is not done before the order is placed, the condition cannot fire.

asNote: TERMINOLOGY: In PP64 the condition field is 'Visit Type' not 'Patient Type' as used in v2 of this document. Confirm the exact field label with your AS before build. VFC mnemonics must be agreed with your clinical informatics team and mapped to the Visit Type dictionary before any Task Generation rule is written.
keyLearning: S6 introduces Result Task Generation, a mechanism that creates additional result tasks based on patient attributes (Visit Type, pathway flags, etc.) rather than who ordered the test or who is currently responsible. This is the foundation for pathway-based routing and is independent of the Radio Button setting. Task Generation applies Trust-wide once configured.

title: General Surgery — OOH: Coverage Schedule Handles It
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Coverage Schedule
schemaFilter: Q7=Yes (OOH/coverage) → ADDS S7–S8, S16–S17, S25, S41
clinicalContext: A consultant orders a CT during the day. The report arrives at 21:00. The on-call registrar is now responsible. Under Path A the coverage schedule automatically routes the result to the on-call registrar. Under Path B the result routes to the original daytime ordering registrar regardless of time.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending — follows coverage schedule automatically. Requesting Clinician — does NOT follow coverage schedule. Fixed to the clinician who placed the order. Coverage Schedule ★ On-call registrar designated as Attending from 17:00. Result at 21:00 → on-call registrar receives automatically. Coverage schedule has no effect on Requesting Clinician routing. The clinician who ordered during the day receives the OOH result. RESULTTASK row IMG.GENSURG (from S3) Deliver To Provider Only Priority Handling Standard (or STAT if OBX-8 = 'C' from S3) Result Task Generation None (in this basic configuration) Workgroup Dictionary No workgroup in basic config (compensation needed for Path B) Ancillary Mapping From S3 (Trust-wide) User Override None Surveillance Rules None
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To In-hours Surgical consultant (Attending during day) — result in consultant's inbox. Ordering registrar — result in registrar's inbox. OOH (21:00) On-call registrar — coverage schedule updates Attending. Radio Button follows. Result in on-call inbox. Ordering daytime registrar — Radio Button does NOT follow coverage. Result still goes to the ordering clinician's inbox even at 21:00.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition SILENT ⚠ Coverage schedule not maintained — Attending field stale or blank. OOH result has no valid target. Daytime ordering clinician goes off shift — result received but not actioned overnight. Not a system failure but a workflow failure. Displaced None when coverage schedule is correct. Daytime clinician receives OOH result but is not in a position to act. Higher risk for critical findings at night. Duplicate None Orphaned Coverage schedule not updated, Attending field points to off-duty clinician
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path B OOH Workgroup: configure an on-call workgroup (E.SURGONCALL) and use Task Generation condition to send results to this workgroup during OOH periods. Requires time-of-day condition in Result Task Generation or a separate RESULTTASK row for OOH service. Path A Coverage schedule maintenance governance: who updates the schedule? Must be a daily operational process, not a one-time configuration.

asNote: ★ OOH SAFETY DIVERGENCE. S7 demonstrates the most clinically significant difference between Path A and Path B in acute inpatient services. Path A handles OOH routing automatically through the coverage schedule. Path B requires an additional workgroup configuration to achieve the same safety outcome. For acute services (surgery, medicine, ED), this scenario should be reviewed with the on-call governance lead before go-live.
keyLearning: Coverage Schedule is the mechanism that updates the Attending field based on time of day, day of week, or shift patterns. Under Path A (Radio Button = Attending), this means results automatically follow the coverage schedule. Under Path B (Radio Button = Requesting Clinician), the coverage schedule has NO effect on result routing — the result always goes to whoever placed the order, regardless of who is now on duty. This is the most important OOH safety distinction between the two paths.

title: Cardiology — Ward Transfer: Result Follows the Patient
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Coverage Schedule / Attending update on transfer
schemaFilter: Q7=Yes (ward transfer is a form of clinician change) | Q2 — assess whether Attending is always populated post-transfer
clinicalContext: An AMU registrar orders an echo. The patient transfers to Cardiology before the report is authorised. The cardiology consultant is now the Attending. Under Path A the result follows the patient automatically. Under Path B the result follows the original ordering clinician (AMU registrar) who is no longer managing the patient.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending — re-evaluates at time of report authorisation. Cardiology consultant is now Attending. Requesting Clinician — fixed at time of order. AMU registrar retains delivery regardless of transfer. RESULTTASK row ★ IMG.CARDIO Deliver To Provider Only Priority Handling Standard (or STAT if OBX-8 = 'C' from S3) Result Task Generation None Workgroup Dictionary No workgroup (Provider Only) — compensation for Path B Ancillary Mapping From S3 (Trust-wide) User Override None Surveillance Rules None
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Cardiology Consultant Patient transferred → Attending updated → report in cardiologist's inbox. Not the requesting clinician → no notification under Provider Only. AMU Registrar Not the Attending → no notification. Correct — no longer managing patient. Original requesting clinician → report in AMU registrar's inbox. Clinician no longer managing patient.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition SILENT ⚠ Transfer not recorded in EPR before report authorised — Attending still AMU registrar, not cardiologist. AMU registrar rotation or absence — S4 pattern re-emerges for transferred patients. Displaced None when transfers are correctly recorded. Result goes to clinician who is no longer responsible — displaced delivery. Duplicate None Orphaned AMU registrar leaves Trust before report arrives (Path B only)
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A Ensure EPR workflow requires Attending update as part of ward transfer process. This is an operational governance requirement, not a configuration item. Path B Cardiology workgroup (E.CARDIO) compensates — even if primary delivery goes to AMU registrar, workgroup ensures cardiology team sees result. Workgroup must include cardiologist(s) on the ward rota.

asNote: The Attending model (Path A) handles ward transfers, rotations, and OOH handovers through the same single mechanism: re-evaluation of the Attending field at time of report authorisation. This is the core architectural advantage of Path A for inpatient services. Under Path B, each of these scenarios requires a compensating workgroup or override.
keyLearning: S8 demonstrates the "result follows the patient" principle under Path A. When a patient transfers between wards or specialties, the Attending field should be updated by the admissions/transfer team. When the radiology report is later authorised, the Radio Button (set to Attending) routes the result to the NEW Attending — the current responsible clinician. Under Path B, the result is frozen to the ORIGINAL ordering clinician regardless of subsequent transfers. This is why Path B requires extensive workgroup compensation in complex inpatient environments.

title: Respiratory — Workgroup and Provider Parallel Delivery
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Workgroup Dictionary
schemaFilter: Q3=Yes (team inbox alongside individual)
clinicalContext: CT chest report goes to the respiratory consultant AND the Respiratory Workgroup (specialist nurses, SpR). Same pattern as S5 applied to a medical specialty.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending (from S1) Requesting Clinician (from S1) RESULTTASK row ★ IMG.RESP Deliver To ★ Workgroup and Provider Workgroup Dictionary ★ E.RESP — Members: specialist nurses, SpR. Triage Type: INPRESULT (inpatient) or AMBRESULT (outpatient Resp clinic) — set per encounter type. Triage Type ★ INPRESULT for inpatient encounters | AMBRESULT for outpatient respiratory clinic Suppress For Clinicians Optional: add respiratory consultants to suppress duplicate (see S5 compensation) Priority Handling Standard (or STAT if OBX-8 = 'C' from S3) Result Task Generation None Ancillary Mapping From S3 (Trust-wide) User Override None Surveillance Rules None
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Primary Individual Respiratory consultant (Attending) — personal inbox via Provider path. Ordering clinician — personal inbox via Provider path. E.RESP Workgroup Team inbox — all specialist nurse and SpR members see report. Team inbox — all specialist nurse and SpR members see report. IDENTICAL workgroup delivery under both paths.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Silent ⚠ None for workgroup delivery — team inbox always receives. None for workgroup delivery — team inbox always receives. Displaced None for workgroup. Individual path: Attending field not updated → stale consultant. Ordering clinician rotated before report — same S4 risk for individual path. Duplicate If the respiratory consultant is also a workgroup member, they receive the result twice (personal + workgroup inbox). Same duplicate risk if ordering clinician is a workgroup member. Orphaned Workgroup has no active members (leave, rotation, empty membership). Covered in S41/S42.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B Workgroup membership must be actively maintained. Assign a named owner (service manager or clinical lead) responsible for adding/removing members. Review membership quarterly. Path A/B To prevent duplicates: use 'Suppress For Clinicians' field in Workgroup Dictionary to suppress the workgroup copy for members who will always receive via the Provider path (e.g., the respiratory consultant if they are listed in the workgroup).

asNote: CRITICAL TERMINOLOGY CORRECTION from v2: Triage Type must match the encounter context. Use INPRESULT for inpatient respiratory ward encounters. Use AMBRESULT for outpatient respiratory clinic encounters. Mixing these causes results to appear in the wrong worklist view. In some builds a single RESULTTASK row cannot support both INPRESULT and AMBRESULT — you may need separate rows for IMG.RESP.IP and IMG.RESP.OP. Confirm with your MEDITECH AS before configuration.
keyLearning: S9 demonstrates the "Workgroup and Provider" delivery pattern, where results are delivered to BOTH an individual clinician (via the Radio Button) AND a shared team inbox (via the Workgroup Dictionary). This is the most common compensation pattern for Path B organisations and provides redundancy for Path A. The workgroup delivery is INDEPENDENT of the Radio Button — it works identically under Path A and Path B. The key configuration decision is "Deliver To = Workgroup and Provider" instead of "Provider Only".

title: ICU — Workgroup and Provider for Critical Care
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Workgroup Dictionary · Coverage Schedule
schemaFilter: Q3=Yes (team inbox essential) | Q7=Yes (frequent handovers)
clinicalContext: Every ICU patient's imaging goes to the ICU consultant (Attending) and the ICU Workgroup. ICU consultants rotate on short ward rounds; the coverage schedule keeps the Attending field current. The workgroup provides continuity across shift changes.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending (from S1) Requesting Clinician (from S1) Coverage Schedule ★ ICU consultant rotation schedule updated daily. Attending field follows current ICU consultant on duty. Coverage schedule not used for individual delivery under Path B. RESULTTASK row ★ IMG.ICU Deliver To ★ Workgroup and Provider (ESSENTIAL — not optional for ICU) Workgroup Dictionary ★ E.ICU — Members: ICU consultants, senior registrars, ICU specialist nurses. Triage Type: INPRESULT. Triage Type ★ INPRESULT (ICU patients are always inpatients) Suppress For Clinicians Optional: suppress for ICU consultants if they prefer single inbox delivery Priority Handling Standard (or STAT if OBX-8 = 'C' from S3) Result Task Generation None (dual delivery via Workgroup and Provider is sufficient) Ancillary Mapping From S3 (Trust-wide) User Override None Surveillance Rules None
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Current ICU Consultant Attending field updated by coverage schedule → current on-duty ICU consultant receives via Provider path. Ordering clinician (may be a registrar or previous shift consultant) receives via Provider path — may not be current ICU consultant. E.ICU Workgroup Team inbox — all ICU team members see report. Provides continuity across shift changes. Team inbox — all ICU team members see report. IDENTICAL workgroup delivery. This compensates for individual delivery risk under Path B.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Silent ⚠ None — workgroup provides redundancy even if Coverage Schedule fails. None — workgroup compensates for individual path risk. Displaced Coverage schedule not updated at shift handover — result goes to off-shift consultant. Workgroup mitigates. Ordering clinician leaves shift — result in unmanned inbox. Workgroup compensates. Duplicate ICU consultant receives result via both Provider and Workgroup paths if they are a workgroup member. Same duplicate risk. Use 'Suppress For Clinicians' to prevent. Orphaned ICU workgroup has no active members during night shift (covered in S41)
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B ICU workgroup membership must be part of the daily ICU shift handover checklist. This is NOT a static configuration — it must be updated as staff come on/off duty, especially during night shifts and weekends. Path A Coverage schedule for ICU must be updated at EVERY shift handover, not just daily. ICU shifts may be shorter than standard ward consultant shifts. Path B ICU workgroup is MANDATORY under Path B. Without it, results ordered by previous shift consultants will never reach the current ICU team.

asNote: ICU is one of the few environments where the workgroup is clinically essential regardless of Path A or B. Ensure the ICU workgroup membership is part of the ICU shift handover checklist and not just a static configuration. In some ICU environments, different workgroups may be needed for day vs night shifts (E.ICU.DAY, E.ICU.NIGHT) to reduce inbox noise for off-duty staff. Discuss this with your ICU clinical lead before go-live.
keyLearning: S10 demonstrates a scenario where "Workgroup and Provider" is not optional — it is a clinical safety requirement. ICU has frequent handovers, complex team structures, and time-critical results. The workgroup provides continuity across shifts. Under Path A, the Coverage Schedule should route individual results to the current ICU consultant; the workgroup is redundancy. Under Path B, the workgroup is the PRIMARY safety mechanism because individual delivery will often go to the wrong clinician. ICU is the strongest use case for mandatory workgroup configuration.

title: ED — Critical Finding: Additional Task to Radiology On-Call
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Ancillary Mapping · Result Task Generation (critical flag) · Workgroup Dictionary
schemaFilter: Q4=Yes (additional recipient on critical flag) | Q5 considered here — critical flag is OBX-8, not surveillance rule
clinicalContext: A CT shows unexpected malignancy. Primary recipient receives STAT priority (via S3 Ancillary Mapping). Additionally, the on-call radiology consultant needs an alert for verbal communication documentation — an RCR/NHS requirement for disclosing unexpected significant findings.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending (from S1) Requesting Clinician (from S1) RESULTTASK row IMG.ED Deliver To Provider Only Priority ★ STAT (OBX-8 critical — from S3) Ancillary Mapping ★ OBX-8 = 'C' or 'U' → STAT priority (Trust-wide from S3) Result Task Generation ★ Critical flag (OBX-8 = C or U) → additional task to E.RADONC regardless of Radio Button E.RADONC Target ★ Radiology on-call workgroup — INPRESULT or RADONCALL Triage Type
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Primary ED recipient ED Attending — STAT in personal inbox. ED requesting clinician — STAT in personal inbox. E.RADONC Workgroup Task Generation fires on OBX-8 critical flag — identical under A and B. Identical — Task Generation is Radio Button-independent.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Silent ⚠ OBX-8 not sent by CRIS for this study type — Ancillary Mapping never fires. Same. Displaced ED Attending has changed since order (ward transfer) — result at STAT goes to new Attending who may not have ordered the scan. Requesting clinician is off shift — STAT result in unmanned inbox.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B The radiology workgroup Task Generation task provides a safety net for both paths — it fires regardless of who the primary recipient is. This is the correct pattern for meeting NHS/RCR requirements for unexpected significant finding communication.

asNote: The Task Generation condition fires on the OBX-8 flag received from CRIS. Confirm OBX-8 values with your RIS/CRIS team before build. Do not assume 'C' — some CRIS systems use 'HH' (critically high) or custom codes. The Ancillary Mapping dictionary must map your CRIS's exact OBX-8 values to the STAT priority trigger.
keyLearning: S11 demonstrates that Result Task Generation creates a safety net that is independent of the Radio Button setting. Both Path A and Path B organisations receive identical workgroup notification when critical findings are flagged. This pattern is essential for regulatory compliance (RCR Standards, NHS patient safety alerts) and should be configured Trust-wide, not service-specific.

title: Paediatrics — Age-Based Safeguarding Task (Trust-Wide Condition)
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Workgroup Dictionary · Result Task Generation (patient age < 18)
schemaFilter: Q4=Yes (safeguarding condition) | Q6=Yes (Caldicott governance applies)
clinicalContext: Any imaging for a patient under 18 triggers an automatic additional task to the Safeguarding Named Nurse workgroup. This condition fires Trust-wide — not just for Paediatrics. A paediatric X-ray, an obstetric USS for a 16-year-old, a trauma CT for a child — all trigger the same condition.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending (from S1) Requesting Clinician (from S1) RESULTTASK row ★ IMG.PAEDS Deliver To Workgroup and Provider Paeds Workgroup ★ E.PAEDS — ward nurses, registrars. Triage Type: INPRESULT Result Task Generation ★ Patient Date of Birth → Age < 18 → additional task to E.SAFEGUARD (Trust-wide, fires for ALL services) E.SAFEGUARD ★ Named Nurse, Safeguarding Team. Triage Type: SAFEGUARD (or INPRESULT if SAFEGUARD not available)
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Primary Individual Paediatric consultant (Attending). Ordering clinician. E.PAEDS Workgroup Team inbox — identical under A and B. Identical. E.SAFEGUARD Workgroup Safeguarding inbox — fires Trust-wide on Age < 18 regardless of Radio Button or service. Identical — completely independent of Radio Button.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Silent ⚠ Date of Birth not populated in EPR — age condition cannot evaluate. Safeguarding task never fires. Same. Duplicate Under-18 patient admitted to adult ward (e.g., 17-year-old on adult surgery) — safeguarding fires appropriately but may cause confusion if not expected by the adult service. Same.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B Ensure Date of Birth is a mandatory field in Registration before imaging orders can be placed. Governance: Caldicott Guardian and Named Nurse must approve the safeguarding workgroup membership and the activation of this condition.

asNote: This is a Trust-wide Result Task Generation condition. It fires for EVERY patient under 18 across EVERY service from the moment it is configured. Ensure the Named Nurse team is prepared for the volume of tasks this will generate. Test extensively before go-live with synthetic paediatric test patients.
keyLearning: S12 demonstrates Trust-wide Task Generation conditions that fire based on patient attributes rather than service-specific configuration. This is extremely powerful for governance (safeguarding, Caldicott, regulatory oversight) but requires careful pre-implementation planning. The Named Nurse team must have capacity and processes in place before activation. This is NOT a silent background process — it is a high-volume operational workflow.

title: Stroke — Hyperacute Pathway via Task Generation
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Result Task Generation (Visit Type = STROKE) · Workgroup Dictionary
schemaFilter: Q4=Yes (pathway routing)
clinicalContext: Urgent CT head/CTA for acute stroke. The primary ED recipient gets the standard task. The HASU workgroup gets an additional task via stroke pathway condition for time-critical thrombolysis decision-making.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending (from S1) Requesting Clinician (from S1) RESULTTASK row IMG.ED Deliver To Provider Only Result Task Generation ★ Visit Type = STROKE → task to E.HASU E.HASU ★ Stroke nurses, thrombolysis nurse, stroke SpR. Triage Type: INPRESULT
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Primary ED recipient ED Attending. ED requesting clinician. E.HASU Workgroup Task Generation fires — identical under A and B. Identical.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Silent ⚠ Visit Type not set to STROKE before order placed — condition never fires, HASU not notified. Time-critical patient safety risk. Same.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B Stroke pathway activation must occur BEFORE imaging is ordered. Ensure the clinical workflow requires Visit Type = STROKE to be set as part of the stroke activation process (typically by the ED charge nurse or triage clinician).

asNote: Stroke is the highest-acuity Task Generation condition in the system. Time from CT to thrombolysis decision depends on this notification reaching the HASU team. Consider whether a telephone/bleep escalation protocol should run in parallel with the Expanse task for the first 6–12 months post go-live while the system is bedded in.
keyLearning: S13 demonstrates pathway-based Task Generation for time-critical clinical conditions. The stroke pathway is the canonical example where Expanse task notification becomes part of a regulated clinical pathway (SSNAP metrics, Royal College standards). Failure to deliver this notification is a patient safety incident. Unlike other scenarios, stroke requires parallel escalation (Expanse + bleep/phone) during early implementation to ensure resilience.

title: Lung Cancer Pathway — MDT Coordinator and CNS
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Result Task Generation (Visit Type = ONC.LUNG) · Workgroup Dictionary
schemaFilter: Q4=Yes (pathway routing)
clinicalContext: Staging CT CAP for suspected lung cancer. Consultant gets standard task. Lung Cancer CNS Workgroup and MDT Coordinator get additional tasks via pathway condition. A single Task Generation condition generates tasks to multiple targets.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending (from S1) Requesting Clinician (from S1) RESULTTASK row IMG.RESP or IMG.ONCO (confirm with clinical lead) Deliver To Provider Only Result Task Generation ★ Visit Type = ONC.LUNG → tasks to E.CANLUNGCNS AND MDT Coordinator (role-based) E.CANLUNGCNS ★ Lung Cancer CNS, key workers. Triage Type: AMBRESULT (outpatient staging pathway) MDT Coordinator ★ Role-based assignment preferred over named user — survives staff changes
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Primary Individual Respiratory/Oncology consultant (Attending). Ordering clinician. E.CANLUNGCNS + MDT Coordinator Task Generation fires — identical under A and B. Role-based MDT Coordinator assignment. Identical.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Silent ⚠ Visit Type not set to ONC.LUNG — condition never fires. CNS and MDT Coordinator not notified. 2WW pathway governance failure. Same. Orphaned MDT Coordinator assigned to a named user who leaves — role-based assignment prevents this if configured correctly. Same.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B Mandate Visit Type entry as part of the 2WW referral acceptance process. Audit Task Generation firing rate monthly against expected 2WW volume as a quality check.

asNote: Prefer role-based MDT Coordinator assignment in Result Task Generation over named user. MEDITECH Expanse allows role lookup — consult your AS on the implementation. Named user assignment requires an update every time the coordinator changes, which creates ongoing maintenance overhead.
keyLearning: S14 demonstrates how a single Task Generation condition can route to multiple recipients (CNS workgroup AND MDT Coordinator). Role-based assignment is critical for sustainability — it decouples the Expanse configuration from individual staff changes. When the MDT Coordinator role is reassigned in the EPR, the task routing updates automatically without requiring AS intervention.

title: IR — Proceduralist, Referrer, and Two Workgroups
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage (two rows) · Workgroup Dictionary (two workgroups)
schemaFilter: Q3=Yes (multiple workgroups) | This scenario corrects a significant error in v2
clinicalContext: IR procedure report must reach the IR consultant, the referring vascular consultant, the IR Team Workgroup, and the Vascular Ward Workgroup. Four recipients require two RESULTTASK rows — one per workgroup. A single RESULTTASK row in Expanse supports only one primary workgroup.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending (from S1) Requesting Clinician (from S1) ★ RESULTTASK Row 1 ★ IMG.IR — Deliver To: Workgroup and Provider → links to E.IR ★ RESULTTASK Row 2 ★ IMG.IR.VASC (second row for same service) — Deliver To: Workgroup and Provider → links to E.VASCWARD E.IR Workgroup ★ IR nurses, fellows, SpR. Triage Type: INPRESULT E.VASCWARD Workgroup ★ Vascular ward nurses, SpR. Triage Type: INPRESULT Referrer Copy Referring vascular consultant receives copy IF referring provider field is populated in the encounter. This is not a workgroup — it uses the standard referring provider delivery path.
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To IR Consultant Attending on IR encounter — personal inbox. Requesting clinician (IR proceduralist) — personal inbox. E.IR Workgroup Row 1 fires — IR team inbox. Identical. E.VASCWARD Workgroup Row 2 fires — vascular ward inbox. Identical. Referring Consultant Via referring provider field — if populated. Identical — referring provider delivery is independent of Radio Button.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Silent ⚠ Referring provider field not populated — referrer copy never generated. Same. Duplicate IR consultant is also a member of E.IR workgroup — receives result twice. Same.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B v2 CORRECTION: A single RESULTTASK row CANNOT deliver to two workgroups simultaneously. Two rows are required. Confirm with your AS that your PP64 build supports multiple RESULTTASK rows per service code before attempting this configuration.

asNote: ★ v2 CORRECTION: The previous version of this document incorrectly stated that a single RESULTTASK row could support multiple workgroups. This is not how Expanse works. Two RESULTTASK rows are required. This is a significant build-time error. Any current configurations based on the v2 guidance should be reviewed.
keyLearning: S15 corrects a critical error from v2 of this guide. Multiple workgroups require multiple RESULTTASK rows. Each row can support ONE workgroup via the 'Deliver To: Workgroup and Provider' setting. This is a hard architectural constraint in Expanse PP64. Attempting to configure multiple workgroups on a single row will result in unpredictable delivery behaviour. Always test multi-workgroup configurations in UAT before go-live.

title: Neurology — Pathway + Coverage Schedule (Epilepsy)
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Result Task Generation · Workgroup Dictionary · Coverage Schedule
schemaFilter: Q4=Yes (pathway) | Q7=Yes (OOH/coverage)
clinicalContext: MRI brain for epilepsy. Consultant in-hours or on-call registrar OOH via coverage, plus Neurology MDT Workgroup via pathway 24/7. The MDT workgroup is not time-dependent — pathway Task Generation fires regardless of hour.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending — follows coverage schedule for OOH. Requesting Clinician — OOH risk: ordering clinician does not change when coverage schedule updates. RESULTTASK row ★ IMG.NEURO — Provider Only Result Task Generation ★ Visit Type = NEURO.EPILEPSY → E.NEUROMDT (fires 24/7 regardless of time or Radio Button) E.NEUROMDT Workgroup ★ Epilepsy nurses, neurophysiology, pharmacist. Triage Type: AMBRESULT (outpatient epilepsy pathway) Coverage Schedule Neurology coverage updates Attending field OOH automatically
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To In-hours individual Neurology consultant (Attending). Ordering clinician. OOH individual On-call registrar via coverage schedule — automatic. Original ordering clinician — NOT on-call registrar. OOH risk. E.NEUROMDT (24/7) Task Generation — always fires. Independent of Radio Button. Identical — Task Generation is Radio Button-independent.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition SILENT ⚠ Coverage schedule not maintained OOH. OOH result goes to off-duty ordering clinician.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path B Add on-call neurology workgroup (E.NEUROONCALL) as a Task Generation target active OOH, OR configure a second RESULTTASK row for OOH periods pointing to the on-call registrar role.

asNote: The pathway workgroup (E.NEUROMDT) fires 24/7 and is completely independent of the Radio Button. This means it provides a consistent safety net for both Path A and B organisations regardless of OOH coverage failures.
keyLearning: S16 demonstrates how Task Generation creates a Radio Button-independent safety net. The MDT workgroup receives every epilepsy pathway result 24/7 regardless of whether the individual delivery succeeds or fails. This architectural pattern is essential for pathway governance.

title: Lymphoma — PET-CT Pathway Routing
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Result Task Generation · Workgroup Dictionary
schemaFilter: Q4=Yes (pathway routing)
clinicalContext: PET-CT for lymphoma staging. Attending (oncologist in-hours or on-call haem registrar OOH) plus Haematology MDT Workgroup via pathway.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending — follows coverage for OOH. Requesting Clinician. RESULTTASK row ★ IMG.NUCMED — Provider Only Result Task Generation ★ Visit Type = ONC.LYMPH → E.HAEMMDT E.HAEMMDT Workgroup ★ MDT coordinator, CNS, pharmacist. Triage Type: AMBRESULT
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Primary Individual Oncologist/on-call via coverage. Ordering clinician. E.HAEMMDT Task Generation — identical under A and B. Identical.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Silent Visit Type = ONC.LYMPH not set before order. Same.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B Haematology MDT workgroup serves as safety net for both paths. Ensure MDT coordinator acknowledges tasks and has a defined turnaround time.

asNote: Six Task Generation conditions now live: VFC, Critical, Age<18, Stroke, Lung Cancer, Lymphoma. Each fires independently. A patient with lymphoma under 18 would trigger both Age<18 (S12) and ONC.LYMPH conditions simultaneously — test this intersection before go-live.
keyLearning: S17 highlights the cumulative nature of Task Generation conditions. Multiple conditions can fire for a single result. A lymphoma patient under 18 triggers safeguarding AND haematology MDT workgroups simultaneously. This intersection behaviour must be tested during UAT.

title: Obstetrics — Safeguarding Fires Automatically for Under-18 (Zero Config)
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Workgroup Dictionary · Result Task Generation — Age<18 condition from S12 fires automatically
schemaFilter: Q4=Yes (but NO new configuration required — cumulative model)
clinicalContext: USS for a pregnant 16-year-old. Standard routing to obstetrician and maternity workgroup. Safeguarding workgroup receives a copy automatically because the Age<18 condition from S12 fires Trust-wide. No new configuration required — this demonstrates the cumulative model.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending (from S1) Requesting Clinician (from S1) RESULTTASK row ★ IMG.OBGYN — Workgroup and Provider E.MATERNITY Workgroup ★ Midwives, obstetric registrars. Triage Type: AMBRESULT (antenatal outpatient) Age<18 Condition Fires automatically from S12. No new configuration. E.SAFEGUARD receives task.
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Primary Individual Obstetrician (Attending). Ordering clinician. E.MATERNITY Workgroup Team inbox — Workgroup and Provider. Identical. E.SAFEGUARD Workgroup Age<18 condition from S12 fires automatically. Zero new config. Identical.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Governance Obstetrics team unaware that safeguarding receives their patient's imaging results. Communication with Named Nurse required. Same.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B Service leads should be informed that the Age<18 Trust-wide condition applies to their service when they go live. Include in go-live communications and patient consent processes.

asNote: Demonstrates the cumulative model: a condition configured in S12 for Paediatrics fires automatically in S18 for Obstetrics with zero additional configuration. The Obstetrics team did not request safeguarding routing — it fires because the Trust-wide condition evaluates age regardless of service.
keyLearning: S18 is a governance warning scenario. Trust-wide conditions (Age<18, Critical Flag) fire across ALL services. When Obstetrics goes live, safeguarding automatically receives imaging results for under-18 patients. Service leads must be informed of this behaviour during go-live planning to prevent governance surprises.

title: Forensic Imaging — Confidentiality via Provider Only + Suppress
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Workgroup Dictionary — Suppress For Clinicians
schemaFilter: Q6=Yes (confidentiality restriction) → ADDS S19–S20 and governance annotation to ALL other scenarios
clinicalContext: Imaging for a sexual assault patient. Results must not appear in any team inbox. Only the attending/ordering ED consultant sees the report. Provider Only delivery plus Suppress For Clinicians if a workgroup was previously configured for ED.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending (from S1) Requesting Clinician (from S1) RESULTTASK row IMG.ED — Provider Only (deliberately, not Workgroup and Provider) Result Task Generation None — no pathway tasks for confidential cases regardless of patient type flags Workgroup Deliberately disabled. If ED normally has a workgroup, this encounter type requires a separate IMG.ED.CONF RESULTTASK row with Provider Only. Suppress For Clinicians ★ Set if any workgroup is associated with this encounter — prevents workgroup visibility. Discuss approach with Caldicott Guardian.
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To ED Consultant Personal inbox only. Personal inbox only (ordering clinician). All workgroups Provider Only blocks workgroup delivery. Suppress reinforces. Same.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Duplicate — Governance Risk If standard ED workgroup (E.ED) is active and this encounter type is not separated into its own RESULTTASK row, result appears in team inbox. Same. SILENT ⚠ Attending is the wrong clinician due to a cover arrangement — confidential result goes to wrong person. Order placed by clerk rather than clinician — result goes to clerk's inbox.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B Create a separate RESULTTASK row (IMG.ED.CONF) for forensic/sexual assault encounter types with Provider Only hardcoded. Do not rely on the standard IMG.ED row being correctly configured for every case. Path A/B Caldicott Guardian must be involved in the design and sign-off of this configuration. Document the decision trail.

asNote: Suppress For Clinicians in the Workgroup Dictionary suppresses the workgroup copy for clinicians who are receiving via the Provider path. Its function is to prevent duplicate delivery — NOT to control confidentiality. Confidentiality is primarily enforced by Provider Only delivery and the absence of a workgroup. Do not rely on Suppress alone for confidential cases.
keyLearning: S19 is a Caldicott governance scenario. Suppress For Clinicians prevents duplicate delivery, but it is NOT a confidentiality control. Confidentiality requires Provider Only delivery plus a separate RESULTTASK row for confidential encounter types. Never assume Suppress alone provides confidentiality.

title: Liaison Psychiatry — Named Exception via User Override
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · User Override Table
schemaFilter: Q6=Yes (named exception) | Q3=No (no workgroup for this service)
clinicalContext: CT head for MH crisis patient. Only the psychiatrist (Attending) and a named MH Liaison Nurse should see the report. No workgroup. User Override grants the named nurse a copy. Caldicott Guardian governance decision.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending (from S1) Requesting Clinician (from S1) RESULTTASK row IMG.ED or IMG.PSYCH — Provider Only User Override Table ★ MH Liaison Nurse added — Deliver To = Provider. Named user receives copy. Workgroup None — no team inbox for psychiatric cases (confidentiality governance).
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Psychiatry Consultant Attending → personal inbox. Ordering clinician → personal inbox. MH Liaison Nurse (named) User Override → personal inbox. Same — User Override is independent of Radio Button.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Orphaned Named nurse leaves — User Override not updated. Result delivered to inactive account. Same. Governance User Override list not reviewed — former staff members retain delivery access. Same.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B User Override Table must be reviewed whenever a staff member in the list changes role or leaves. Assign ownership to the service lead or MH Liaison coordinator. Include in leaver checklist.

asNote: User Override Table is a per-user configuration, not a per-service one. Every entry is tied to a specific named person. This is appropriate for small, stable named exceptions but is not scalable for large teams. For larger groups, use a workgroup instead.
keyLearning: S20 demonstrates User Override for named exceptions. This mechanism is appropriate for small, stable teams (1-3 individuals) but creates maintenance overhead. When a named individual leaves, the User Override entry becomes orphaned. For larger teams, use workgroups with role-based membership instead.

title: Renal — STAT + Workgroup + Coverage Combined at 21:00
mechanisms: All Level 1-2 mechanisms simultaneously
schemaFilter: Q7=Yes + Q3=Yes + Q4=Yes (critical flag from S11)
clinicalContext: OOH imaging with critical finding. Three paths fire simultaneously using pre-existing infrastructure: on-call renal registrar (coverage), Renal Workgroup, and STAT priority + radiology alert (S11). Zero new Trust-wide configuration required.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending — on-call at 21:00 via coverage. Requesting Clinician — ordering clinician from earlier in day. RESULTTASK row ★ IMG.RENAL — Workgroup and Provider + STAT via Ancillary Mapping E.RENAL Workgroup ★ Renal nurses, dialysis team. Triage Type: INPRESULT Critical Task Generation OBX-8 critical → E.RADONC (from S11 — fires regardless of primary routing)
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To OOH Primary Individual On-call renal registrar via coverage schedule. Ordering daytime clinician. E.RENAL Workgroup STAT in team inbox — identical under A and B. Identical. E.RADONC Critical Task Generation fires — identical under A and B. Identical.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition SILENT ⚠ Coverage schedule not updated — STAT result to off-duty renal consultant. OOH result to unmanned daytime inbox. Workgroup is primary safety net.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B Three mechanisms provide redundant safety nets. Even if one fails (coverage stale, inbox unmanned), the workgroup and radiology alert provide backup. This is the target state for all high-acuity inpatient services.

asNote: S21 demonstrates the power of the cumulative model: three separate mechanisms configured across Levels 1-3 all fire simultaneously without any new Trust-wide configuration. Each mechanism was built for a different service but provides cross-service benefit.
keyLearning: S21 shows how multiple independent mechanisms built in previous scenarios combine to create a highly resilient notification system. The cumulative model means each new service benefits from infrastructure built for others. This is the architecture that enables safe OOH critical result delivery without manual intervention.

title: Addendum Re-Notification — Trust-Wide Toggle
mechanisms: Re-notify on Addendum (MIS Customer Defined Parameters — CDS Parameters tab)
schemaFilter: All services — this is a Trust-wide governance decision
clinicalContext: When a radiologist issues an amended report, all original recipients must be re-notified. This single toggle in CDS Parameters re-evaluates all delivery rules for the amended report — including the current Attending if the patient has transferred since the original.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Re-notify on Addendum ★ YES — changed in CDS Parameters. Path A: re-notification goes to CURRENT Attending (who may differ from original if patient transferred). ★ YES — same toggle. Path B: re-notification goes to original requesting clinician (fixed at order time — does not update on addendum). All other settings Unchanged. Original delivery rules re-fire.
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To All original recipients Re-notification goes to CURRENT Attending — may differ from original recipient if patient transferred or Attending changed. Re-notification goes to original requesting clinician — same person as original notification regardless of patient movement.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Displaced Patient transferred to a specialty not involved in the original imaging — new Attending receives unexpected addendum. Original requesting clinician no longer responsible — receives addendum out of context. Duplicate High-volume addendum practice in radiology → inbox noise for Attendings. Same for requesting clinicians.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B Consider selective rather than universal Re-notify on Addendum. Discuss with Radiology Clinical Lead: should addenda only re-notify when they change clinical significance? This is a governance decision, not a configuration option in PP64.

asNote: NAVIGATION: CDS Parameters is accessed via Management Information Systems (MIS) > Customer Defined Parameters > CDS Parameters tab. Confirm exact tab label in your PP64 build — this may vary slightly between patch levels. Re-notify on Addendum is a significant patient safety toggle with Trust-wide impact. Requires CCIO sign-off before activation.
keyLearning: Re-notify on Addendum is one of the most consequential single-toggle decisions in the entire results notification architecture. Path A re-evaluates to current Attending (following patient transfers), Path B remains fixed to original requesting clinician. Both approaches have clinical justification — discuss with your Radiology and Clinical Governance teams before activation.

title: Colorectal Staging — Surveillance Rule for Pulmonary Nodules
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Result Task Generation · Workgroup Dictionary · MIS Surveillance Rules (text-content matching)
schemaFilter: Q5=Yes (text-content trigger) → ADDS S23-S24
clinicalContext: CT CAP staging for colorectal cancer. Colorectal MDT workgroup via pathway. IF the report text contains 'pulmonary nodule', Lung Cancer CNS also notified via Surveillance Rule. Text-content matching operates on the report free text as indexed by Expanse — this is separate from the clinical Surveillance module.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending (from S1) Requesting Clinician (from S1) RESULTTASK row IMG.ONCO — Provider Only Result Task Generation ★ Visit Type = ONC.COLORECTAL → E.CANCOLORECTAL ★ Surveillance Rule ★ IF report text contains 'pulmonary nodule' OR 'lung nodule' → additional task to E.CANLUNGCNS. Fires regardless of Radio Button setting.
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Primary Individual Oncologist (Attending). Ordering clinician. E.CANCOLORECTAL Task Generation — identical under A and B. Identical. E.CANLUNGCNS (conditional) ONLY if report contains 'pulmonary nodule'. Surveillance Rule fires — identical under A and B. Identical.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Duplicate — False Positive Report contains 'no pulmonary nodule seen' or 'no evidence of pulmonary nodule' — text match fires on the negative finding. Lung Cancer CNS receives unnecessary alert. Same. Silent — False Negative Radiologist uses different terminology ('lung nodule', 'pulmonary lesion', 'subcentimetre opacity') — Surveillance Rule does not fire. Same.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B Surveillance Rules for text matching require clinical governance review with Radiology Lead before activation. Maintain a controlled vocabulary list: agree with radiology which exact terms should trigger the rule. Test against at least 50 real historical reports before go-live. Review quarterly for false positive/negative rates.

asNote: ARCHITECTURE CLARIFICATION from v2: Text-content Surveillance Rules (this scenario) are configured within MIS as a results-routing mechanism, separate from the clinical Surveillance module in Decision Support. They operate on indexed report text. Confirm with your MEDITECH AS exactly which module and screen in PP64 implements text-content result routing — the implementation varies across patch levels.
keyLearning: Text-content Surveillance Rules are powerful but imperfect. They enable cross-pathway notifications (colorectal patient → lung cancer CNS) but are vulnerable to both false positives (negative findings triggering alerts) and false negatives (terminology variation). Use with clinical governance oversight and controlled vocabulary agreements with reporting radiologists.

title: DEXA — Value-Based Surveillance: T-Score Threshold
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Workgroup Dictionary · Value-based Surveillance (HL7 OBX numeric threshold)
schemaFilter: Q5=Yes (value threshold trigger)
clinicalContext: DEXA scan. If T-score normal: consultant + Rheum WG only. If T-score ≤ -2.5: also Falls Prevention and Fracture Liaison Service. The T-score value is received via HL7 OBX segment from the DEXA reporting system.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending (from S1) Requesting Clinician (from S1) RESULTTASK row ★ IMG.RHEUM — Workgroup and Provider E.RHEUM Workgroup ★ Coordinator, specialist nurse. Triage Type: AMBRESULT (outpatient bone health clinic) ★ Value-based Surveillance ★ IF OBX value (T-score) ≤ -2.5 → additional tasks to E.FALLSPREV and E.FLS. Threshold is a clinical governance decision reviewed annually.
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Primary Individual + E.RHEUM Standard delivery — always. Standard delivery — always. E.FALLSPREV + E.FLS (conditional) ONLY if T-score ≤ -2.5. Value-based rule fires — identical under A and B. Identical.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Silent DEXA reporting system does not send T-score as a discrete OBX value — rule cannot evaluate. Confirm HL7 message structure with DEXA equipment vendor before build. Same. Governance T-score threshold not reviewed annually — clinical threshold may become outdated against NICE guidance. Same.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B Confirm OBX segment structure from DEXA equipment HL7 interface before building this rule. The T-score may arrive as OBX-5 with OBX-3 test code — confirm exact mapping with your AS and radiology physics team.

asNote: Value-based Surveillance Rules require a discrete numeric value in the HL7 message — they cannot evaluate free text descriptions. If T-score is only present in the narrative report text, a text-based Surveillance Rule (S23 pattern) would be required instead. Confirm before build.
keyLearning: Value-based Surveillance Rules are more precise than text-based rules (S23) because they operate on discrete numeric data from HL7 OBX segments. However, they depend on the sending equipment transmitting structured data — not all imaging modalities send discrete values. Always confirm HL7 message structure with equipment vendors before configuring value-based rules.

title: Cross-Site Transfer — Attending Follows to New Site
mechanisms: Radio Button · Coverage Schedule · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Workgroup Dictionary
schemaFilter: Q7=Yes (cross-site transfer is a form of Attending change)
clinicalContext: Patient transfers from Site A to Site B. Attending updated to Site B consultant. Under Path A, report routes automatically to Site B consultant. Under Path B, result still goes to Site A ordering clinician.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending — re-evaluates on transfer. Now points to Site B consultant. Requesting Clinician — fixed to Site A clinician at order time. Does not update on transfer. All other settings Unchanged — same RESULTTASK rows and workgroups as S5
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Site B Consultant Attending updated on transfer → report in Site B inbox. Not the requesting clinician → no notification under Provider Only. Site A Ordering Clinician Not the Attending → no notification. Correct. Original requesting clinician → still receives result. May not be managing patient.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Silent Transfer not recorded in EPR → Attending still points to Site A consultant. Site A ordering clinician is no longer responsible — displaced result.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B Inter-site transfer process must include EPR Attending update as a mandatory step. This is an operational governance requirement. Path B Site B workgroup (E.ORTHOINPT.SITEB or equivalent) configured and included in Workgroup and Provider delivery ensures Site B team receives result regardless of ordering clinician location.

asNote: Path A handles cross-site transfers through the same Attending re-evaluation mechanism as ward transfers (S8), rotations (S4), and OOH (S7). This is the core advantage of Path A in multi-site NHS Trusts. Under Path B each of these scenarios requires a compensating workgroup.
keyLearning: S25 is the "Stale Attending" scenario referenced in the task. It demonstrates how Path A's Attending re-evaluation mechanism automatically handles cross-site transfers, while Path B requires workgroup compensation. For multi-site NHS Trusts, this is one of the most important architectural differences between the two paths. Path A follows clinical responsibility dynamically; Path B requires compensating infrastructure.

title: Locum on AMU — User Override Safety Net
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Workgroup Dictionary · User Override Table
schemaFilter: Q2=Not always (locum may not have coverage schedule) | Q3=Yes (workgroup safety net)
clinicalContext: Locum consultant orders CTPA. Under Path A the locum becomes the Attending. Under Path B the locum is the requesting clinician. In both cases, the permanent team should also see the result — the locum may not be contactable after their session ends.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending — locum is Attending during their session. Requesting Clinician — locum placed the order. RESULTTASK row IMG.AMU (from S2) Deliver To Provider Only (standard for AMU) ★ User Override Table ★ Locum user record: Deliver To = Workgroup. Forces E.AMU workgroup to receive a copy in addition to individual delivery. Applies regardless of Path A or B. E.AMU Workgroup ★ AMU permanent team workgroup. Triage Type: INPRESULT
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Locum Consultant Personal inbox (Attending). Personal inbox (requesting clinician). E.AMU Workgroup User Override forces workgroup copy — ensures permanent team receives result. Same — User Override is independent of Radio Button.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition SILENT ⚠ Locum not registered in User Override Table before starting shift — no workgroup copy generated. Permanent team not notified. Orphaned Locum's Expanse account deactivated after placement ends before all reports are authorised.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B OPERATIONAL PROCESS REQUIRED: Medical Staffing must notify the Application Specialist (or designated admin) of every new locum before their first shift. A standing User Override entry must be created in advance. Include in locum booking confirmation workflow. Path A/B Consider a generic 'locum' user profile in Expanse with User Override → Workgroup hardcoded, so any locum signed in under the generic profile automatically triggers workgroup delivery. Discuss with your AS — this has access control implications.

asNote: User Override Table entries must be proactively maintained. Retrospective entry after a locum has started will not cover reports already in the system. Assign ownership of User Override maintenance to a named individual — typically the rota coordinator or medical staffing team lead.
keyLearning: Locum safety nets require proactive User Override configuration before the locum starts their shift. Neither Path A nor Path B automatically compensates for temporary clinicians without additional workgroup delivery.

title: Multi-Stage — Wet Read, Final Report, Addendum
mechanisms: Radio Button · Coverage Schedule · Re-notify on Addendum (S22) · RESULTTASK Item Triage
schemaFilter: Q7=Yes (multi-stage delivery) | Q2 — Attending changes at each stage
clinicalContext: Urgent CT head: preliminary report (Stage 1) to ED Attending, final report (Stage 2) to AMU consultant after admission, addendum (Stage 3) re-notifies via S22. In MEDITECH Expanse, a 'preliminary' or 'wet read' depends on report status — the radiology team must authorise a preliminary report before a RESULTTASK fires.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending — re-evaluates at EACH stage. S1: ED consultant. S2: AMU consultant (patient admitted). S3: AMU consultant or new Attending if transferred. Requesting Clinician — FIXED at order time. S1, S2, and S3 all go to the original ordering clinician regardless of patient movement. Re-notify on Addendum YES (from S22) — S3 re-evaluates current Attending YES — S3 goes to original requesting clinician, not current responsible clinician. All other settings Per service at time of task firing
tables.outcome: Stage Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Stage 1 (preliminary) ED Attending — patient in ED. Ordering ED clinician — same as Stage 1. Stage 2 (final) AMU consultant — patient admitted, Attending updated. STILL the original ED ordering clinician — not the AMU consultant. Stage 3 (addendum) Current Attending — whoever is responsible at time of addendum. Original ordering clinician — may not be managing patient at Stage 3.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition SILENT ⚠ Stage 1 preliminary report not formally authorised in Expanse/CRIS — RESULTTASK never fires for wet read. Clinically dangerous for acute presentations. Displaced None when Attending is correctly maintained. Stages 2 and 3 displaced to non-responsible clinician.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path B Workgroup on AMU (E.AMU) compensates for Stage 2 and 3 displacement — AMU team sees report via workgroup even though primary delivery goes to ED ordering clinician. Path A/B Confirm with Radiology that preliminary/wet read reports are formally authorised in CRIS with a 'Preliminary' status — only authorised reports trigger RESULTTASK generation. Verbal or informal wet reads do not.

asNote: In Expanse, RESULTTASK generation is triggered by report authorisation status in the IDM/Radiology module. A report must reach an 'Authorised' or 'Verified' state (preliminary or final) to trigger delivery. Confirm the authorisation workflow with your Radiology team — some trusts only authorise final reports, which means no preliminary notification occurs.
