keyLearning: Multi-stage reporting with patient movement between stages demonstrates the core architectural difference between Paths A and B. Path A re-evaluates Attending at each stage, following the patient. Path B remains fixed to the original ordering clinician across all stages.

title: IBD Service — Triple Routing: Attending + Workgroup + Pathway
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Workgroup Dictionary · Result Task Generation
schemaFilter: Q3=Yes + Q4=Yes (triple routing)
clinicalContext: MRI small bowel for IBD. Consultant (Attending/ordering), IBD Nurse Workgroup (primary action owners), and IBD MDT Coordinator via pathway condition. Three recipients via three different mechanisms.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending Requesting Clinician RESULTTASK row ★ IMG.GASTRO — Workgroup and Provider E.IBDNURSE Workgroup ★ IBD nurse specialists. Triage Type: AMBRESULT (outpatient IBD clinic) Result Task Generation ★ Visit Type = IBD → E.IBDMDT (MDT coordinator, pharmacist)
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Primary Individual GI consultant (Attending). Ordering clinician. E.IBDNURSE Workgroup Workgroup and Provider delivery — identical under A and B. E.IBDMDT Task Generation — identical under A and B.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Duplicate GI consultant is also an E.IBDNURSE workgroup member — double delivery.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B Use Suppress For Clinicians on E.IBDNURSE if the GI consultant is a member and is already receiving via Provider path.

asNote: Triple routing via three mechanisms: Radio Button (individual), Deliver To (workgroup), Task Generation (pathway). This is the standard NHS shared care pathway pattern. Apply to any service with a CNS-led workgroup model plus MDT coordination.
keyLearning: Triple routing demonstrates how multiple Expanse mechanisms work in parallel. The Radio Button determines individual delivery, Deliver To controls workgroup delivery, and Task Generation adds pathway-specific recipients. All three fire simultaneously and independently.

title: Major Trauma — Multiple Workgroups Fire Simultaneously
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Result Task Generation (TRAUMA condition — 4 workgroups) · Ancillary Mapping (STAT if critical)
schemaFilter: Q3=Yes + Q4=Yes + Q5 possible (if critical OBX-8)
clinicalContext: Whole-body CT for polytrauma. Trauma pathway generates tasks to four specialty workgroups simultaneously. Three of the four workgroups already exist from earlier scenarios — demonstrates the cumulative model at scale.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending (trauma team leader) Requesting Clinician (ED clinician) RESULTTASK row IMG.ED — Provider Only + STAT if critical (S3) ★ Result Task Generation ★ Visit Type = TRAUMA → E.TRAUMAED + E.ORTHOINPT (from S5) + E.GENSURG + E.ICU (from S10) — four workgroups, one condition E.TRAUMAED ★ New — trauma nurses, trauma coordinator. Triage Type: INPRESULT
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Primary Individual Trauma team leader (Attending). Ordering ED clinician. E.TRAUMAED, E.ORTHOINPT, E.GENSURG, E.ICU All four receive simultaneous tasks from single Task Generation condition. Three of four workgroups existed before this scenario. Identical under A and B.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Silent Visit Type = TRAUMA not set during resuscitation phase — trauma team may not have time to update EPR. Overload All four workgroups receive simultaneous notifications — potential inbox overload during major incident.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B Pre-configure trauma activation as a one-click EPR order set that automatically sets Visit Type = TRAUMA alongside the imaging orders. Eliminates the manual step during time-critical resuscitation. Path A/B Major incident plan: consider suppressing non-critical workgroups (ortho, surgery) for major incidents and routing only to E.TRAUMAED initially. Add as a configurable option in the trauma activation order set.

asNote: A single Task Generation condition can fire to multiple workgroups simultaneously. This is the most complex single Result Task Generation condition in the guide. Test extensively with synthetic test patients before go-live. Ensure all four workgroups have active members before activating the trauma condition.
keyLearning: The trauma scenario demonstrates the power of cumulative configuration. Most workgroups receiving trauma results were built in earlier scenarios (E.ORTHOINPT, E.ICU). Only E.TRAUMAED is new. This is how complex pathways are assembled from simple building blocks.

title: Lung Cancer Inpatient at 20:00 — All Mechanisms Active (Level 3 Synthesis)
mechanisms: All Level 1–3 mechanisms active simultaneously
schemaFilter: All schema questions answered — demonstrates full Level 3 configuration
clinicalContext: Staging CT on Friday at 20:00. Coverage schedule, lung cancer pathway, respiratory workgroup, surveillance rule for nodules, STAT if critical, re-notify if amended. Every mechanism from Levels 1–3 potentially fires. Zero new configuration required — all infrastructure built in S1–S29.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending — on-call at 20:00 via coverage. Requesting Clinician — ordering clinician earlier that day. Re-notify on Addendum YES (S22) — re-evaluates current Attending if amended. YES — still routes to original requesting clinician on addendum. All other mechanisms Per previous scenarios — all fire automatically from cumulative configuration.
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To On-call registrar (20:00) Coverage schedule → Attending. Workgroup and Provider → on-call inbox. Not the requesting clinician → no individual delivery. Respiratory workgroup compensates. E.RESP Workgroup Workgroup and Provider (S9) — always fires. E.CANLUNGCNS + MDT Coord Task Generation: ONC.LUNG (S14) — identical under A and B. Cross-pathway (conditional) Surveillance Rule (S23) — if 'pulmonary nodule' in report.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition Multiple Coverage stale → on-call not reached. Workgroup compensates. On-call not reached individually. Workgroup is primary safety net.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B At Level 3 completion, multiple compensating mechanisms ensure delivery even when individual routing fails. The system is resilient — not because any single mechanism is perfect but because multiple independent mechanisms provide redundancy.

asNote: S30 is the Level 3 synthesis scenario. S45 (Level 5) repeats this with full Level 4 and 5 mechanisms also active, demonstrating that Path A and B achieve equivalent outcomes at full maturity through different mechanism combinations.
keyLearning: S30 demonstrates system resilience through layered redundancy. Coverage schedule handles OOH, workgroups provide safety nets, pathway routing adds MDT coordination, and surveillance rules catch cross-pathway findings. No single mechanism is perfect, but their combination is robust.

title: ★ NEW — GP Direct Access Plain Film — No Attending in EPR
mechanisms: Radio Button — BOTH PATHS FAIL · User Override Table · Referring Provider Field · External notification
schemaFilter: Q1 → External referrer/GP answers NEITHER Path A nor Path B → Path C (Fallback) critical
clinicalContext: A GP uses the Trust's direct access imaging pathway to order an X-ray. There is no inpatient encounter — no Attending is assigned. The GP is not an Expanse user in most cases. Both Path A and Path B fail to resolve to the correct recipient without additional configuration.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting ★ Radio Button FAILS — No Attending assigned on a GP direct access encounter. Delivery target: blank or defaults to a system user. FAILS — GP is the requesting clinician but may not have an Expanse inbox. Result cannot be delivered to external user. ★ Path C — Fallback No Attending = result orphaned or sent to fallback system account. SILENT FAILURE. Must be compensated. GP user account not in Expanse = result delivery fails or bounces. SILENT FAILURE. Compensation — Referring Provider Field If GP is registered as a Referring Provider in Expanse (some trusts do this for direct access GPs), the referring provider delivery path can be used. Compensation — Reporting Workgroup ★ Create a dedicated GP Direct Access reporting workgroup (E.GPDIRECTACCESS) staffed by a GP liaison nurse or admin coordinator who manages external GP notification. External Notification GP notification may need to occur outside Expanse entirely — via NHS e-Referral result letter, RIS report dispatch, or GP system interface.
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To GP (intended recipient) FAILS under standard Path A — no Attending. E.GPDIRECTACCESS workgroup notified instead. FAILS under standard Path B unless GP has Expanse account. E.GPDIRECTACCESS workgroup compensates. E.GPDIRECTACCESS Workgroup Compensation workgroup receives result. Coordinator dispatches to GP via agreed external channel.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition SILENT ⚠ — BOTH PATHS No compensation workgroup and GP not an Expanse referring provider. Result lost in system account inbox. GP never notified. Governance GP notification process not defined — compensation workgroup receives result but no protocol for dispatching to GP.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B MANDATORY: Map all GP direct access imaging encounters to a dedicated compensation workgroup BEFORE go-live. Define the external GP notification pathway in your operational policy. Direct access imaging without a resolved notification pathway is a patient safety risk that neither global setting resolves automatically.

asNote: ★ NEW SCENARIO. GP direct access imaging is common in NHS Trusts and represents a genuine gap in both Path A and Path B standard configurations. This must be explicitly addressed in go-live planning. Confirm with your Radiology Manager whether a RIS-to-GP notification interface (e.g., via NHS e-Referral or GP IT system integration) exists and document it as the primary notification pathway.
keyLearning: S31 is one of the few scenarios where both Path A and Path B fail without explicit compensation. External referrers (GPs) are outside the Expanse user model. A dedicated workgroup with an operational dispatch protocol is essential for patient safety.

title: ★ NEW — AHP Extended Scope — Physiotherapist-Ordered MSK Imaging
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · User Role configuration for AHP
schemaFilter: Q1 → Requesting Clinician is the correct primary recipient for AHP-ordered imaging → Path B CORRECT, Path A FAILS
clinicalContext: A physiotherapist with extended scope ordering rights requests an MSK X-ray. The result should return to the physiotherapist. There is no consultant Attending on an AHP direct access encounter. Path B (Requesting) is the correct architecture for this service. Path A will not resolve to the correct recipient.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting ★ Radio Button FAILS — No consultant Attending on AHP direct access encounter. Result orphaned or directed to fallback account. CORRECT — Physiotherapist is the requesting clinician. Result delivered to physiotherapist's inbox IF their Expanse role and inbox are correctly configured. AHP Role Configuration Not relevant — Path A does not resolve for this encounter type. ★ Physiotherapist must have: (1) Active Expanse user account, (2) Provider role or equivalent with inbox, (3) Registered as ordering clinician in the encounter. Confirm with your AS before go-live. AHP Workgroup E.PHYSIO workgroup can compensate for Path A failure. Primary delivery to individual physiotherapist + workgroup for team visibility.
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Physiotherapist FAILS — no Attending → no individual delivery. E.PHYSIO workgroup compensates if configured. CORRECT — result in physiotherapist's inbox. E.PHYSIO Workgroup Compensation — workgroup receives result. Physiotherapist sees result via team inbox. Additional copy in team inbox for peer review and governance.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition SILENT ⚠ No AHP workgroup configured — result orphaned. High risk. AHP not configured as a Provider role in Expanse — inbox not created. Result delivery fails.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A AHP workgroup (E.PHYSIO) is mandatory for all AHP-ordered imaging under Path A — it is the only delivery mechanism that works for this encounter type. Path B Ensure AHP user accounts are configured with Provider role and inbox BEFORE go-live for direct access ordering. Work with your AS and HR/Medical Staffing to create Expanse user accounts for all AHP extended scope practitioners.

asNote: ★ NEW SCENARIO. AHP extended scope ordering is increasingly common in NHS MSK, respiratory, and mental health services. Path B (Requesting) is architecturally correct for these services. Path A organisations MUST compensate with a workgroup. AHP user account configuration is often overlooked in EPR go-live projects — address this explicitly in your go-live readiness checklist.
keyLearning: S32 demonstrates a scenario where Path B is architecturally superior to Path A. AHP direct access ordering has no consultant Attending, making Path A fundamentally incompatible without workgroup compensation. This is a critical consideration for NHS trusts expanding AHP roles.

title: ★ NEW — Outpatient Rotating Consultant Clinic — Matching Result to the Right Clinician
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Attending assignment in OP encounters
schemaFilter: Q1 — critical difference: in OP, is the Attending always the correct clinician or is the ordering clinician safer?
clinicalContext: Three consultants share a clinic across the week. Patient seen by Dr A on Monday; order placed. Report authorised on Friday when Dr B is in clinic and happens to be assigned as Attending due to an EPR scheduling error. Under Path A the result goes to Dr B. Under Path B the result correctly goes to Dr A who ordered the investigation.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting ★ Radio Button Attending — IF EPR scheduling/Attending assignment is accurate, correct clinician receives result. IF EPR has admin error (Dr B assigned when Dr A saw patient), WRONG clinician receives result. Requesting Clinician — result always goes to Dr A who placed the order. More reliable for OP rotating clinic environments where Attending assignment may have admin errors. All other settings Unchanged from service standard
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Ordering Consultant (Dr A) Only if Dr A is Attending at time of report authorisation — EPR admin dependency. Always — Dr A is the requesting clinician regardless of EPR Attending assignment. Wrong Consultant (Dr B) IF EPR error assigns Dr B as Attending — result goes to Dr B silently. Not the requesting clinician → no individual delivery to Dr B. Risk of displacement gone.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition SILENT ⚠ EPR scheduling admin error assigns wrong Attending — result silently delivered to incorrect clinician. Dr A never receives result. HIGH RISK in high-rotation OP clinics. None for individual delivery — always goes to ordering clinician. Displaced Correct only when EPR Attending assignment is accurate. None for individual delivery.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A For high-rotation OP clinics, consider switching to Path B at the service level using a second RESULTTASK row with Requesting model OR using User Override to force ordering clinician delivery for known rotating services. However: the global Radio Button cannot be changed per service — discuss this limitation with your AS. Path A/B Strengthen EPR Attending assignment governance for OP clinics. Ensure scheduling admin staff understand the patient safety implications of incorrect Attending assignment under Path A.

asNote: ★ NEW SCENARIO. This is an important NHS-specific scenario because many trusts run shared OP clinics with rotating consultants. The global Radio Button cannot be changed per service — it is Trust-wide. If your Trust uses Path A and has rotating OP clinics, you must either improve EPR Attending assignment governance OR accept the silent failure risk and compensate with workgroups.
keyLearning: S33 exposes a critical Path A vulnerability in rotating outpatient clinics where EPR admin errors can silently misdirect results to the wrong consultant. Path B is immune to this failure mode because it follows the ordering clinician regardless of subsequent Attending assignment changes.

title: ★ NEW — Discharge Before Report Authorisation — Patient Safety Critical
mechanisms: Radio Button · Coverage Schedule · Re-notify on Addendum · Workgroup Dictionary — discharge safety net
schemaFilter: Q2 → NOT ALWAYS resolvable → CRITICAL — adds S34 to ALL reading lists for inpatient services
clinicalContext: Patient admitted for investigation, imaged on day 2, discharged on day 3. Radiologist authorises the report on day 5. There is no active Attending — the patient is no longer in the hospital. Under Path A the Attending field is blank or points to the discharge summary clinician. Under Path B the ordering inpatient clinician has rotated. BOTH paths are at risk of silent failure. This is the most clinically dangerous scenario in this document.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting ★ Radio Button Attending — Attending field may be blank, cleared on discharge, or pointing to the admitting clinician who has no active role. RESULT MAY BE ORPHANED. Requesting Clinician — inpatient ordering clinician may have rotated or left by day 5. Inbox not actively monitored for this patient. RESULT MAY BE STRANDED. ★ Path C — Fallback behaviour If Attending is blank at time of authorisation: Expanse behaviour depends on PP64 configuration — may deliver to a system default account, may silently drop the task, or may log an error. Confirm with your AS what happens to reports with no valid Attending. If requesting clinician account is inactive: delivery fails silently. No bounce notification generated. Compensation — Discharge Workgroup ★ Configure a dedicated 'Discharged Patient Results' workgroup (E.DISCHRESULTS) — every report for a patient with no active Attending is routed here via Result Task Generation condition. Staff this workgroup with a clinical admin coordinator. Compensation — GP Notification ★ For significant findings in discharged patients, GP notification must be part of the operational workflow. Expanse result routing to GP inbox requires GP account or referring provider configuration (S31 pattern).
tables.outcome: Recipient Path A — Result Goes To Path B — Result Goes To Primary Individual Blank or stale Attending — ORPHANED RESULT. PATIENT SAFETY RISK. Rotated or departed ordering clinician — STRANDED RESULT. PATIENT SAFETY RISK. E.DISCHRESULTS Workgroup Compensation: Task Generation condition on patient discharge status routes to workgroup.
tables.failures: Failure Type Path A — Trigger Condition Path B — Trigger Condition SILENT ⚠ — BOTH PATHS — PATIENT SAFETY CRITICAL Attending blank post-discharge. No delivery target. Report lost. Patient may never receive clinically significant findings. Ordering clinician inactive or departed. Delivery fails silently. Orphaned Even if delivered to a stale Attending inbox, the result may not be acted upon if the Attending is no longer managing the patient. Rotated clinician may not recognise the significance of a result for a patient they no longer see.
tables.compensation: Applies To Compensation Strategy Path A/B — MANDATORY Every NHS Trust using Expanse for inpatient imaging MUST have a documented process for managing reports that are authorised after patient discharge. Options: (1) Discharge workgroup with clinical coordinator, (2) Radiology department holds and dispatches post-discharge results manually, (3) GP notification interface for all post-discharge significant findings. This is a clinical governance requirement — include in pre-go-live sign-off checklist.

asNote: ★ NEW SCENARIO — PATIENT SAFETY CRITICAL. The discharge-before-report scenario is the highest-priority patient safety gap in both Path A and Path B. It must be explicitly addressed before go-live. Trusts without a compensation mechanism for this scenario should NOT go live with Expanse imaging result routing without escalating to their CCIO and Clinical Risk team. Include in the SI (Serious Incident) risk register pre-go-live.
keyLearning: S34 is the most dangerous scenario in this guide. Neither Path A nor Path B has a built-in mechanism to handle reports that arrive after patient discharge. A dedicated discharge results workgroup with an active clinical coordinator is the only reliable compensation. This MUST be in place before go-live.

title: ★ NEW — 2WW GP Referral — External Referrer Notification Limits
mechanisms: Radio Button · RESULTTASK Item Triage · Result Task Generation · External notification pathway
schemaFilter: Q1 → External referrer (GP) needs notification | Both paths partially fail | S31 pattern + pathway routing
clinicalContext: GP refers patient on a 2WW colorectal pathway. Hospital generates the imaging order. Both the referring GP and the hospital consultant need the result. The GP may not have an Expanse inbox. Hospital routing follows the standard Path A or B pattern; GP notification must occur via an external channel.

tables.configuration: Configuration PATH A — Radio Button = Attending PATH B — Radio Button = Requesting Radio Button Attending — hospital consultant receives result via standard Path A. Requesting Clinician — hospital ordering clinician receives result. Referring Provider Field If GP is registered as Referring Provider in encounter — referring provider delivery path may work IF GP has Expanse account. Result Task Generation ★ Visit Type = ONC.COLORECTAL → E.CANCOLORECTAL (from S23). GP notification is a separate operational step. ★ External GP notification Via NHS e-Referral result notification, GP system interface, or manual letter dispatch. NOT via Expanse internal routing in most configurations.

★ Surveillance Rule

Fires on partial text match including negations — 'no pulmonary nodule' matches the string 'pulmonary nodule'. No negation logic in basic text-match Surveillance Rules.

E.CANLUNGCNS inbox flooded. CNS stops reading alerts. TRUE POSITIVES missed in the noise. Complete loss of clinical utility.

Duplicate — False Positive Storm

tables.compensation:
Applies To Compensation Strategy

Path A/B

BEFORE ACTIVATING ANY SURVEILLANCE RULE: (1) Test against minimum 100 real historical reports (anonymised). (2) Measure false positive rate — target <5%. (3) Agree acceptable false positive rate with clinical lead and CNS team. (4) Document the controlled vocabulary agreed with Radiology (exact phrases that DO and DO NOT trigger). (5) Review monthly for first 3 months post go-live.

Path A/B

If PP64 supports negation logic in Surveillance Rules, use it. Confirm with your AS. If not, consider using only highly specific phrases unlikely to appear in negative statements.

asNote: ★ NEW SCENARIO. Surveillance Rule false positive storms are a well-documented implementation failure mode. They cause alert fatigue and can cause true positives to be missed — which is worse than not having the rule at all. Clinical governance sign-off and pre-live testing are mandatory, not optional.

Path A/B — END STATE

At Level 5 full configuration, Path A and Path B achieve EQUIVALENT CLINICAL OUTCOMES through different mechanism combinations. Path A relies primarily on the Attending mechanism + coverage schedule, with workgroups as secondary safety net. Path B relies primarily on workgroups as primary mechanism, with User Override and Task Generation providing additional layers. The global setting is NOT the most important decision. The maturity and maintenance of the surrounding configuration is.

asNote: S45 is the synthesis scenario. It demonstrates that the choice of Path A vs Path B is recoverable at any maturity level IF the compensating mechanisms are correctly configured and operationally maintained. The goal is Level 5 configuration for all high-acuity services. This does not happen at go-live — it is achieved incrementally across the five maturity levels described in this document.
